query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "fashionchameleon: towards real-time and interactive human-garment vide" computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on task offloading and resource allocation in Vehicular Edge Computing (VEC), which is a distinct domain from the user's question regarding real-time human-garment video processing and interactive fashion applications. While both involve "real-time" systems, the paper does not measure the same biological, physical, or algorithmic mechanisms (e.g., garment segmentation, pose estimation, or interactive rendering) nor does it address the specific empirical population or variables
